title: haloragis heterophylla var. filiformis
intro: Haloragis heterophylla var. filiformis, also known as the 'jointed glasswort' or 'jointed haloragis', is a perennial plant species in the family Haloragaceae, native to Australia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Haloragis heterophylla var. filiformis include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and provide relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant is used in various herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves and stems in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
